channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: European Commission Internal Security Fund CEEP 2026: Up to €1.5M Grants for Civil Society Projects Combating Online Extremism
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The EU Internal Security Fund (ISF) CEEP Grant 2026 offers up to €1.5 million in funding for civil society-led projects aimed at preventing online radicalisation and strengthening community resilience across Europe. Designed for organisations working at the intersection of youth engagement, digital safety, and social cohesion, this opportunity supports collaborative, high-impact initiatives across multiple EU […]

The post European Commission Internal Security Fund CEEP 2026: Up to €1.5M Grants for Civil Society Projects Combating Online Extremism appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The EU Internal Security Fund (ISF) CEEP Grant 2026 offers up to €1.5 million in funding for civil society-led projects aimed at preventing online radicalisation and strengthening community resilience across Europe. Designed for organisations working at the intersection of youth engagement, digital safety, and social cohesion, this opportunity supports collaborative, high-impact initiatives across multiple EU countries.

About the Program

The Community Engagement and Empowerment Programme (CEEP), funded under the Internal Security Fund (ISF), is a European Commission initiative focused on preventing and countering radicalisation that can lead to violent extremism and terrorism.

Building on previous EU efforts such as the Civil Society Empowerment Programme (CSEP) and the Radicalisation Awareness Network (RAN), this call supports projects that address the growing risks associated with online radicalisation—particularly among youth and vulnerable communities.

The program encourages evidence-based, participatory approaches that combine both online and offline interventions. Projects are expected to actively involve communities in their design and implementation, ensuring that solutions are grounded in real-world needs and experiences.

With a total budget of €5 million, the call will fund several large-scale projects that demonstrate strong collaboration, innovation, and long-term impact.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Online spaces are increasingly being used to spread extremist ideologies, often targeting young people through sophisticated and emotionally engaging content. Addressing this challenge requires more than top-down interventions—it demands community-driven, inclusive, and sustainable solutions.

This funding opportunity recognizes the critical role that civil society organisations play in prevention efforts. By supporting cross-border collaboration, the program enables organisations to share knowledge, scale effective practices, and respond more strategically to evolving threats.

It also reflects a broader shift in EU policy toward prevention, resilience-building, and youth empowerment as central pillars in combating radicalisation.

What Participants Will Gain

This grant provides both financial and strategic benefits for participating organisations.

Selected projects will receive substantial funding—ranging from €1 million to €1.5 million per project—with up to 90% of eligible costs covered. This allows organisations to implement ambitious, multi-year initiatives without bearing the full financial burden.

Beyond funding, participants will gain the opportunity to:

  • Build strong international partnerships across at least three EU Member States
  • Engage directly with youth, educators, and community leaders in co-created initiatives
  • Develop and scale innovative approaches to digital resilience and prevention
  • Strengthen organisational capacity in research, monitoring, and evaluation
  • Increase visibility and credibility through EU-supported programming

Projects will also benefit from structured frameworks for impact measurement, ensuring that outcomes are not only achieved but clearly demonstrated.

Who Can Apply

This opportunity is open to consortia of organisations rather than individual applicants.

To be eligible, applications must include at least three organisations from three different EU Member States. Importantly, at least two of these must be civil society organisations.

Eligible participants may include non-profits, NGOs, research institutions, and other relevant entities working in areas such as youth engagement, education, digital safety, or community development.

Denmark is not eligible to participate in this call.

Eligible Areas

Projects must be implemented within EU Member States and may also involve relevant priority third countries, depending on the scope and partnerships.

The program supports initiatives aligned with the following priority areas:

  • Digital resilience and critical thinking online
  • Strengthening community trust and social cohesion
  • Addressing multiple forms of extremism, including jihadist and far-right narratives
  • Youth empowerment and peer-led prevention strategies
  • Inclusive, co-created approaches involving affected communities

Application Process and Deadline

Applications must be submitted through the EU Funding & Tenders Portal using a structured online system.

The submission process involves registering all participating organisations, preparing a detailed project proposal (including technical description, budget, and supporting documents), and uploading all required materials before the deadline.

Proposals are evaluated based on three main criteria:

  • Relevance (30 points) – alignment with program objectives and EU priorities
  • Quality (50 points) – methodology, feasibility, and consortium strength
  • Impact (20 points) – expected outcomes, sustainability, and scalability

To be considered for funding, proposals must score at least 21 out of 30 in relevance and a minimum of 70 out of 100 overall.

The final deadline to apply is 27 May 2026 at 17:00 CET. Late submissions will not be accepted.

See all the requirements and apply on the official European Commission website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post European Commission Internal Security Fund CEEP 2026: Up to €1.5M Grants for Civil Society Projects Combating Online Extremism appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Spärck AI Scholarship 2026 at The University of Manchester: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity for Future AI Leaders
item.dc:creator: Hope N. Magwira
item.description:
The University of Manchester has announced an exciting and highly competitive opportunity for aspiring artificial intelligence professionals worldwide — the Spärck AI Scholarship 2026. This prestigious UK government-backed initiative is designed to attract top talent from across the globe to pursue advanced studies in AI and related disciplines at the master’s level. Named in honor […]

The post Spärck AI Scholarship 2026 at The University of Manchester: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity for Future AI Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The University of Manchester has announced an exciting and highly competitive opportunity for aspiring artificial intelligence professionals worldwide — the Spärck AI Scholarship 2026. This prestigious UK government-backed initiative is designed to attract top talent from across the globe to pursue advanced studies in AI and related disciplines at the master’s level.

Named in honor of Karen Spärck Jones, a pioneer in natural language processing and artificial intelligence, the scholarship represents a significant investment in the next generation of AI innovators and leaders.

 

What is the Spärck AI Scholarship?

The Spärck AI Scholarship is a flagship programme introduced by the UK government in collaboration with leading universities. The aim is to:

  • Attract exceptional students globally
  • Strengthen the UK’s AI ecosystem
  • Develop future leaders in artificial intelligence

The University of Manchester is among only nine elite UK institutions selected to offer this scholarship, making it a highly sought-after opportunity.

 

What Does the Scholarship Cover?

Successful applicants will receive a comprehensive funding package, including:

  1. Full Tuition Fee Waiver
  • Covers the entire cost of your master’s degree
  1. Generous Living Stipend
  • Annual maintenance stipend equivalent to the UKVI minimum
  • £20,780 for the 2025/26 academic year
  1. Exclusive Academic and Professional Benefits

As a Spärck AI Scholar, you will:

  • Join a world-class cohort of high-aching, innovative students
  • Gain access to industry exposure and networking opportunities
  • Participate in structured cohort activities
  • Build connections with professionals across AI sectors

 

Why This Scholarship Stands Out

This is more than just financial support — it’s a career-defining experience.

Key Advantages:

  • Leadership Development: Build skills to lead in AI innovation
  • Industry Integration: Exposure to real-world AI applications
  • Career Pathways: Opportunity to apply for AI-related work placements
  • Global Network: Become part of a prestigious community of AI leaders

Towards the end of your programme, you may apply for a work placement with one of the scholarship’s industry partners, giving you hands-on experience in the AI field.

 

Eligible Master’s Programmes

The scholarship applies to the following MSc courses starting in September 2026:

  • MSc Artificial Intelligence
  • MSc Machine Learning
  • MSc Robotics
  • MSc Advanced Computer Science
  • MSc Cyber Security

 

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the Spärck AI Scholarship, applicants must meet the following requirements:

Academic Requirements

  • Hold a First-class honours degree (70% or equivalent)
  • Degree must be in Computer Science or a closely related field

Admission Requirement

  • Must have a conditional or unconditional offer from the University of Manchester for one of the eligible MSc programmes

Personal Attributes

Applicants must demonstrate:

  • A strong motivation to contribute to the UK AI ecosystem
  • Interest in being part of a government-funded initiative
  • A clear drive to maximize this opportunity

 

Selection Process

Candidates will be assessed based on:

  1. Academic excellence
  2. Quality of scholarship application
  3. Performance in the interview process

Final decisions will be announced by 15 June 2026.

 

Personal Statement Requirements

Applicants must submit a compelling personal statement that highlights:

  • Vision for AI technologies and their future impact
  • Curiosity, critical thinking, and problem-solving ability
  • Adaptability in fast-changing environments
  • Strong communication and teamwork skills
  • Leadership potential and entrepreneurial mindset

Additionally, candidates must respond to a chosen essay topic related to AI.

 

How to Apply

Click here to learn more and to apply

Follow these steps carefully:

  1. Apply for admission to one of the eligible MSc programmes
  2. Secure a conditional or unconditional offer
  3. Eligible candidates will be contacted via email with instructions to apply for the scholarship

 

Application Deadline

  • Scholarship Application Deadline: 30 April 2026
  • Final Decision Notification: By 15 June 2026

Diversity and Inclusion

The University of Manchester strongly encourages applications from individuals of all backgrounds, especially those from underrepresented groups. The selection process focuses on:

  • Academic potential
  • Commitment to inclusivity
  • Contribution to a supportive scholar community

For more global scholarship opportunities on OFY, click here

The post Spärck AI Scholarship 2026 at The University of Manchester: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity for Future AI Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: WIA Young Leaders Programme 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Women Leaders to Gain Global Exposure, Mentorship and High-Level Networking
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The Women in Africa (WIA) Young Leaders Programme is one of the most prestigious leadership initiatives designed specifically to identify, empower, and elevate young African women who are already making an impact across various sectors. Organized by Women in Africa Philanthropy, this highly competitive and fully funded program seeks to cultivate a new generation of […]

The post WIA Young Leaders Programme 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Women Leaders to Gain Global Exposure, Mentorship and High-Level Networking appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Women in Africa (WIA) Young Leaders Programme is one of the most prestigious leadership initiatives designed specifically to identify, empower, and elevate young African women who are already making an impact across various sectors. Organized by Women in Africa Philanthropy, this highly competitive and fully funded program seeks to cultivate a new generation of influential women leaders who will shape the future of the African continent.

Each year, thousands of applications are received, yet only a select few exceptional candidates are chosen—making it one of the most competitive leadership fellowships in Africa.

The program is not just a recognition of excellence; it is a transformative journey that provides access to elite networks, global exposure, mentorship, and advanced leadership training.

About the WIA Young Leaders Programme

The WIA Young Leaders Programme was created to support and highlight outstanding African women who demonstrate leadership potential, innovation, and a strong commitment to Africa’s development.

It operates under the broader mission of Women in Africa Philanthropy, which aims to accelerate women’s leadership and entrepreneurship across the continent by providing access to knowledge, networks, and opportunities.

The program partners with globally recognized institutions and companies such as Dior, Lazard, and KPMG to deliver world-class experiences and opportunities to selected participants.

Why This Programme Matters

Africa is home to one of the highest rates of female entrepreneurship globally, yet women remain underrepresented in leadership positions. The WIA Young Leaders Programme addresses this gap by:

  • Identifying high-potential women leaders
  • Providing them with global exposure and leadership tools
  • Connecting them to influential decision-makers
  • Amplifying their visibility and impact

This initiative is part of a broader effort to ensure women play a central role in Africa’s economic and social transformation.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

The WIA Young Leaders Programme has clearly defined eligibility criteria to ensure it targets the most impactful candidates.

1. Age Requirement

Applicants must be between 25 and 40 years old, although some flexibility may be considered based on exceptional experience.

2. Nationality and Origin

Applicants must:

  • Be born in an African country, OR
  • Have at least one parent born in an African country

3. Geographic Eligibility

  • Open to candidates living in Africa or abroad
  • African diaspora applicants are fully eligible

4. Language Requirements

  • Applicants are ideally:
    • Anglophone (English-speaking)
    • Francophone (French-speaking)
    • Lusophone (Portuguese-speaking)
  • Multilingual candidates are strongly preferred

5. Professional Background

Candidates must already demonstrate:

  • Strong leadership potential
  • Proven impact in their field (entrepreneurship, policy, research, etc.)
  • Active involvement in Africa’s development

6. Personal Qualities

Applicants are expected to embody:

  • Humility
  • Empathy
  • Resilience
  • Vision for Africa’s future
  • Ability to take on high-level responsibilities

Target Audience (Who This Opportunity Is For)

This program is specifically designed for:

  • Young African women entrepreneurs
  • Professionals and executives
  • Policy leaders and public sector actors
  • Researchers and innovators
  • Civil society leaders
  • Social impact changemakers

In short, it is for women who are already making a difference and are ready to scale their impact globally.

Benefits of the WIA Young Leaders Programme

The benefits of this fellowship extend far beyond financial support. It offers a holistic leadership development experience.

1. High-Level Networking Opportunities

Participants gain access to:

  • CEOs and top executives
  • Government ministers
  • Global business leaders
  • Influential journalists and public figures

2. Personalized Mentorship

Each selected leader is paired with an international mentor who provides:

  • Strategic guidance
  • Career development support
  • Leadership insights

3. Advanced Training Programs

Participants receive:

  • Board-level training
  • Media and communication coaching
  • Leadership and career development sessions
  • Customized training tailored to individual needs

4. Global Visibility

Winners benefit from:

  • Media exposure
  • Promotion through WIA platforms
  • Recognition as top African women leaders

5. International Learning Expedition

One of the most exciting aspects is the Learning Expedition, which includes:

  • A fully funded trip to a major global city (often Paris)
  • Participation in high-level workshops
  • Meetings with global leaders and innovators
  • Exposure to international best practices

Programme Timeline (Typical Cycle)

Although timelines may vary slightly each year, the general process includes:

Application Phase

  • Opens: May
  • Closes: June (e.g., June 19, 2025)

Selection Process

  • June – July: Application review and shortlisting
  • July – August: Interviews with an international jury

Final Selection

  • September: Official announcement of selected leaders

Learning Expedition

  • November – December: International leadership experience

Selection Criteria

Candidates are evaluated based on:

  • Academic and professional achievements
  • Leadership experience and potential
  • Demonstrated impact on society
  • Vision for Africa’s future
  • Commitment to driving change

The selection process is rigorous, ensuring only the most outstanding candidates are chosen.

Why You Should Apply

The WIA Young Leaders Programme is more than just a fellowship—it is a gateway to global leadership. By joining this program, you:

  • Become part of an elite network of African women leaders
  • Gain international exposure and recognition
  • Access mentorship from global experts
  • Accelerate your career and impact

With only a limited number of slots available each year, this opportunity is highly competitive and prestigious.

Apply here

Deadline

3rd May 2026

If you are a young African woman with a strong track record of leadership and a vision for transforming Africa, the WIA Young Leaders Programme is one of the most powerful opportunities available today. It combines funding, mentorship, training, and global exposure into a single life-changing experience designed to elevate Africa’s next generation of women leaders.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post WIA Young Leaders Programme 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for African Women Leaders to Gain Global Exposure, Mentorship and High-Level Networking appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Dignified Menstruation Media Fellowship 2026: A Global Opportunity for Journalists to Drive Change Through Storytelling.
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The conversation around menstruation is evolving—but not fast enough. Across the globe, menstruation remains deeply stigmatized, misunderstood, and often dismissed as a private issue rather than recognized as a fundamental human rights concern. The Dignified Menstruation Media Fellowship 2026, organized by Global South Coalition for Dignified Menstruation, is a powerful initiative designed to shift this […]

The post Dignified Menstruation Media Fellowship 2026: A Global Opportunity for Journalists to Drive Change Through Storytelling. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The conversation around menstruation is evolving—but not fast enough. Across the globe, menstruation remains deeply stigmatized, misunderstood, and often dismissed as a private issue rather than recognized as a fundamental human rights concern. The Dignified Menstruation Media Fellowship 2026, organized by Global South Coalition for Dignified Menstruation, is a powerful initiative designed to shift this narrative through impactful storytelling and media advocacy.

This fellowship is not just another professional opportunity—it is a call to action for journalists to reshape global discourse, expose systemic inequalities, and highlight the intersection of menstruation with human rights, gender-based violence, climate justice, and social equity.

 

Why Dignified Menstruation Matters

Dignified Menstruation is an emerging global framework that goes far beyond access to menstrual products. While mainstream discussions often focus on sanitary products or taxation, this approach emphasizes:

  • Human dignity and rights
  • Gender equality
  • Freedom from discrimination and violence
  • Access to education and health
  • Intersection with climate justice and systemic inequality

Despite its importance, media coverage has historically been narrow, often ignoring the deeper structural issues that perpetuate menstrual discrimination. This fellowship aims to bridge that gap by equipping journalists with the tools and knowledge needed to tell more comprehensive and impactful stories.

 

Fellowship Objectives

The program is built around two primary goals:

  1. Strengthening Knowledge
    • Equip journalists with a deep understanding of dignified menstruation as a human rights issue.
    • Explore its connections to systemic inequalities, patriarchy, and social justice.
  2. Driving Policy and Advocacy Impact
    • Use media storytelling to influence policymakers.
    • Highlight the urgency of integrating dignified menstruation into global and national policies.

 

Expected Outcomes

Participants in the fellowship will contribute to meaningful global change by:

  • Developing a nuanced understanding of menstrual discrimination
  • Producing research-based, high-impact media stories
  • Encouraging policy integration across sectors, including climate justice
  • Amplifying engagement among:
    • Governments
    • NGOs and INGOs
    • Academic institutions
    • Donors and development partners

 

Fellowship Activities

Selected fellows will undergo a structured and immersive experience:

1. Intensive Online Training

  • Duration: 3 days
  • Schedule: Up to 5 hours per day
  • Focus areas:
    • Dignified Menstruation frameworks
    • Ethical journalism practices
    • Research and storytelling techniques

2. Research & Story Development

  • Fellows will create in-depth, evidence-based media content
  • Topics will explore intersections with:
    • Human rights
    • Climate justice
    • Sexual and Reproductive Health and Rights (SRHR)
    • Education and public health

3. Continuous Mentorship

  • Ongoing coaching and editorial support
  • Guidance throughout the fellowship period

 

Fellowship Benefits

  • Financial Support:
    • $300 stipend awarded upon successful completion of the fellowship
  • Professional Growth:
    • Access to global experts and networks
    • Enhanced storytelling and investigative skills
  • Global Exposure:
    • Opportunity to publish impactful stories on international platforms

 

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be a journalist working in international media, including:
    • Television
    • Online platforms
    • Newspapers
    • Magazines
  • Demonstrate experience in covering topics such as:
    • Social justice
    • Human rights
    • Climate justice
    • SRHR
    • Health or education

 

Application Process

To apply, candidates must submit:

  1. Completed application form
  2. Detailed resume
  3. Portfolio of past work
  4. Letter of interest, clearly outlining:
    • Motivation for applying
    • Commitment to the fellowship
    • Intended contribution

Selection Process:

  • Applications are reviewed based on eligibility and quality
  • Shortlisted candidates may be contacted for additional information
  • Final selections are communicated via email

 

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship is ideal for journalists who want to:

  • Tell stories that challenge harmful norms and stigma
  • Influence policy and public discourse
  • Contribute to a global movement for dignity and equality
  • Build expertise in a rapidly emerging human rights field

Click Here to learn more about this opportunity and to apply.

Key Dates and Timeline

  • Application Announcement: 17 April 2026
  • Application Deadline: 20 May 2026
  • Participant Confirmation: 1 June 2026
  • Training Dates: 15–17 June 2026
  • Fellowship Duration: June – October 2026

Important Note: Incomplete applications will be automatically disqualified.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities here

The post Dignified Menstruation Media Fellowship 2026: A Global Opportunity for Journalists to Drive Change Through Storytelling. appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded TAGDev 2.0 Scholarships 2026/2027: Study Agriculture and Transform Africa’s Future
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The call for applications for the TAGDev 2.0 Scholarship Programme for the 2026/2027 Academic Year is now officially open, presenting a powerful opportunity for ambitious students across Africa to pursue higher education in agriculture and related fields. This initiative is implemented by the Regional Universities Forum for Capacity Building in Agriculture (RUFORUM) in partnership with […]

The post Fully Funded TAGDev 2.0 Scholarships 2026/2027: Study Agriculture and Transform Africa’s Future appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The call for applications for the TAGDev 2.0 Scholarship Programme for the 2026/2027 Academic Year is now officially open, presenting a powerful opportunity for ambitious students across Africa to pursue higher education in agriculture and related fields. This initiative is implemented by the Regional Universities Forum for Capacity Building in Agriculture (RUFORUM) in partnership with the Mastercard Foundation, both of which are committed to strengthening Africa’s agricultural sector through education, innovation, and youth empowerment.

This scholarship programme is designed not only to support academic excellence but also to develop a new generation of transformative leaders equipped to tackle food insecurity, unemployment, and sustainable development challenges across the continent.

About the TAGDev 2.0 Scholarship Programme

The Transforming African Agricultural Universities to Meaningfully Contribute to Africa’s Growth and Development (TAGDev 2.0) programme focuses on enhancing agricultural education and training by supporting students financially and academically.

Through this programme, scholars gain access to quality education, leadership development, and practical skills that are directly aligned with Africa’s development priorities.

The initiative places a strong emphasis on inclusivity and equitable access to education, encouraging applications from underrepresented and vulnerable groups.

Participating Universities

Applicants can choose from a wide range of reputable African universities participating in the TAGDev 2.0 programme. These institutions are known for their strong academic programs in agriculture, science, and technology.

The partner universities include:

  • Gulu University
  • Egerton University
  • Africa University
  • University of the Free State
  • Malawi University of Science and Technology
  • University of Eldoret
  • Uganda Martyrs University
  • University of Port Harcourt
  • University of Cape Coast
  • University of Bamenda
  • National University of Agriculture (English and French tracks available)
  • Université Mohammed VI Polytechnique

Each university may have specific programme offerings, eligibility criteria, and application procedures, so applicants are encouraged to review the individual university requirements carefully.

Who Should Apply?

The TAGDev 2.0 Scholarship is open to both male and female applicants. However, the programme strongly encourages applications from:

  • Women seeking to advance in agricultural sciences
  • Refugees and internally displaced persons
  • Persons living with disabilities
  • Individuals from marginalized or underserved communities

This inclusive approach ensures that the benefits of higher education reach those who need it most, contributing to broader social and economic transformation.

Key Benefits of the TAGDev 2.0 Scholarship

While specific benefits may vary slightly by institution, selected scholars can generally expect:

  1. Comprehensive Financial Support
    • Coverage of tuition fees
    • Accommodation and living expenses
    • Study materials and research support
  2. Leadership and Mentorship Opportunities
    • Training in leadership, entrepreneurship, and community engagement
    • Access to mentorship from industry and academic experts
  3. Hands-On Agricultural Training
    • Practical exposure through internships, fieldwork, and innovation projects
    • Skills development tailored to real-world agricultural challenges
  4. Networking and Career Development
    • Opportunities to connect with professionals, researchers, and fellow scholars across Africa
    • Enhanced employability and career readiness

Application Process

Interested applicants must apply directly through the respective partner universities. Each institution provides detailed guidelines outlining:

  • Admission requirements
  • Eligible courses
  • Required documentation
  • Application procedures

It is crucial to carefully follow the instructions provided by your chosen university to ensure a successful application.

Important Deadline

  • Submission Deadline: 1st May 2026

Applicants are strongly advised to submit their applications well before the deadline to avoid last-minute challenges.

Why You Should Apply

The TAGDev 2.0 Scholarship is more than just financial aid—it is a transformative opportunity to:

  • Gain access to world-class education in agriculture
  • Contribute to solving Africa’s food security challenges
  • Build a meaningful and impactful career
  • Become part of a network of change-makers across the continent

If you are passionate about agriculture, innovation, and sustainable development, this scholarship could be your gateway to making a lasting difference.

Click here to learn more and apply.

Discover more global Scholarship opportunities on OFY here

The post Fully Funded TAGDev 2.0 Scholarships 2026/2027: Study Agriculture and Transform Africa’s Future appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: AFRIKA KOMMT! 15th Intake Bosch Fellowship 2026–2028: Fully Funded Career-Defining Opportunity in Germany for African Professionals
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
*The AFRIKA KOMMT! 15th Intake Fellowship (2026–2028) is one of the most prestigious and competitive international opportunities for African professionals seeking global exposure, leadership development, and hands-on corporate experience in Germany. This detailed guide explores everything you need to know about the Bosch fellowship profiles under the programme—covering eligibility, benefits, application process, deadlines, and career […]

The post AFRIKA KOMMT! 15th Intake Bosch Fellowship 2026–2028: Fully Funded Career-Defining Opportunity in Germany for African Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
*The AFRIKA KOMMT! 15th Intake Fellowship (2026–2028) is one of the most prestigious and competitive international opportunities for African professionals seeking global exposure, leadership development, and hands-on corporate experience in Germany. This detailed guide explores everything you need to know about the Bosch fellowship profiles under the programme—covering eligibility, benefits, application process, deadlines, and career outcomes.

What is AFRIKA KOMMT!?

AFRIKA KOMMT! (translated as “Africa is coming!”) is a flagship initiative of German industry designed to empower young African leaders by connecting them with top German corporations. Since its inception in 2008, the programme has built a strong network of professionals across Africa and Europe, promoting long-term economic collaboration.

The fellowship is structured to provide:

  • Intensive German language training
  • Advanced leadership and management development
  • An 8-month corporate placement in a leading German company
  • Long-term professional networking opportunities

This is not just a training programme—it is a transformational leadership journey designed to create globally competitive African professionals.

Bosch Fellowship Profiles (15th Intake)

Under the 15th intake, Robert Bosch GmbH offers several specialized fellowship roles tailored to different professional backgrounds.

1. Product Specialist – African Mobility Aftermarket Team

This role focuses on automotive products, marketing, and business development.

Key Requirements:

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in economics, marketing, business, or related technical fields
  • Experience in marketing, sales, or automotive sector
  • Skills in product development and business strategy

Career Path:
After the fellowship, candidates may transition into permanent roles in Bosch offices in Africa (e.g., Johannesburg, Cairo, Casablanca).

2. Technical Market Access Fellow

This position blends engineering expertise with regulatory and market analysis.

Key Requirements:

  • Degree in Mechanical or Electrical Engineering
  • Knowledge of technical regulations and project management
  • Ability to translate technical standards into market strategies

3. Sales & Marketing Specialist – Industrial Boilers

This role targets professionals in industrial engineering and B2B business development.

Key Requirements:

  • Master’s degree in engineering, economics, or management
  • 2–5 years of experience in sales, marketing, or industrial sectors
  • Strong analytical and cross-functional teamwork skills
  • Professional proficiency in English and French (additional languages are an advantage)

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

The AFRIKA KOMMT! Fellowship is designed for high-potential African professionals who demonstrate leadership and ambition.

General Eligibility

Applicants must:

  • Be citizens of an African country
  • Be under 35 years old
  • Hold a university degree in a relevant field
  • Have 2–5 years of professional experience
  • Possess excellent English language skills

Bosch-Specific Eligibility (Country Restrictions)

Due to visa and placement considerations, Bosch profiles are limited to applicants from specific countries:

  • Morocco
  • Egypt
  • South Africa

Applicants must either:

  • Be nationals of these countries, or
  • Already have legal rights to live and work there

Target Audience (Who This Opportunity is For)

This fellowship is ideal for:

  • Young African professionals in engineering, business, IT, and industrial sectors
  • Individuals with leadership potential and international career ambitions
  • Professionals seeking corporate experience in Europe
  • Candidates committed to driving development in Africa

Fields and Disciplines Covered

The Bosch fellowship and wider AFRIKA KOMMT! programme span multiple industries:

  • Engineering and Manufacturing
  • Automotive Technology
  • Sales and Marketing
  • Business Development
  • Project Management
  • Digital Transformation and Innovation

Benefits of the Fellowship

The AFRIKA KOMMT! programme is fully funded, meaning selected fellows receive comprehensive support, including:

  • International travel costs
  • Monthly stipend
  • Accommodation in Germany
  • Health insurance
  • Language and leadership training

Additional benefits include:

  • Real-world experience in global corporations
  • Exposure to German business culture
  • Access to a powerful alumni network across Africa and Europe

Programme Structure

The fellowship follows a structured three-phase model:

1. Preparation Phase

  • Selection process and onboarding
  • Initial German language training

2. Fellowship in Germany

  • Intensive language course
  • Leadership training modules
  • 8-month corporate placement

3. Alumni Phase

  • Continued networking
  • Professional development opportunities
  • Participation in AFRIKA KOMMT! initiatives

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship stands out because it:

  • Offers direct placement in top global companies
  • Builds practical skills, not just academic knowledge
  • Provides a clear pathway to international careers
  • Strengthens Africa–Germany economic collaboration

Many alumni go on to become:

  • Industry leaders
  • Entrepreneurs
  • Senior managers in multinational organizations

Application Process

To apply successfully:

  1. Review the available Bosch fellowship profiles carefully
  2. Select the role that best matches your qualifications
  3. Prepare required documents:
    • CV
    • Academic transcripts
    • Motivation letter
    • References
  4. Submit your application online before the deadline

Applicants are strongly advised to follow the official step-by-step application guide provided on the programme website.

Apply here

Deadline

10th May 2026

The AFRIKA KOMMT! 15th Intake Bosch Fellowship 2026–2028 is more than just an international programme—it is a gateway to global leadership, professional excellence, and long-term career transformation. With its fully funded structure, strong corporate partnerships, and focus on African talent development, this fellowship remains one of the most impactful opportunities available to young professionals across the continent.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post AFRIKA KOMMT! 15th Intake Bosch Fellowship 2026–2028: Fully Funded Career-Defining Opportunity in Germany for African Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: InTent Internship Programme (IIP) 2026: Fully Sponsored Summer Internship for Students Worldwide
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Are you a Bachelor’s, Master’s, MBA, or PhD student looking for a fully funded summer internship in 2026? The InTent Internship Programme (IIP) 2026 offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience while contributing to real-world solutions in sustainability, innovation, and social impact. This prestigious programme connects talented students with purpose-driven startups and NGOs working […]

The post InTent Internship Programme (IIP) 2026: Fully Sponsored Summer Internship for Students Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a Bachelor’s, Master’s, MBA, or PhD student looking for a fully funded summer internship in 2026? The InTent Internship Programme (IIP) 2026 offers a unique opportunity to gain hands-on experience while contributing to real-world solutions in sustainability, innovation, and social impact.

This prestigious programme connects talented students with purpose-driven startups and NGOs working toward a fairer, nature-positive, and more resilient world.

 Key Details (At a Glance)

  • Programme: InTent Internship Programme (IIP) 2026
  • Duration: 2–3 months (Summer 2026)
  • Eligibility: Bachelor’s, Master’s, MBA, and PhD students
  • Location: On-site or virtual (depending on organisation & legal requirements)
  • Start Dates: Between June and July 2026
  • End Date: Latest 15 October 2026
  • Application Deadline: Mid-May 2026

The InTent Internship Programme (#IIP) 2026 is calling all students to apply for a summer internship in one of the 24 selected purpose-driven organisations. Join a fully sponsored 2–3 months summer internship to advance your career and contribute to long-term impact towards a fairer, nature-positive and more resilient world.

The IIP connects impact-driven Bachelor, Master, or MBA students from leading universities with Startups and NGOs innovating for a fairer, nature-positive and more resilient world through sponsored summer internships. This programme provides an opportunity for passionate and talented interns to drive participating organisations to the next level and develop solutions for challenging issues.

Key Facts

What: A max 3-month fully paid internship for a Bachelor, Master, MBA or PhD student during the summer of 2026

Who: Bachelor, Master or MBA students from different study fields based on the project needs

When: From 15th June to 1st July 2026 to latest 15th October 2026

Location: The organisation’s offices or virtual depending on location and local legal requirements

What Students Get

  • A fully sponsored 2–3 month summer internship
  • Hands-on experience within an organisation dedicated to creating tangible positive impact
  • Expand your network with InTent’s ecosystem of purpose-driven organisations
  • Advance your career in sustainability by working directly with experts addressing the world’s most pressing challenges
  • Full support across your internship journey and beyond

Key Dates

Mid April — Recruitment starts

Mid May 2026 — Application ends

5th June / 1st July 2026 — Start of internship

16th July 2026 (virtual) — Kick-off ceremony

30th September 2026 — End of programme period

 

“Our mission is to empower students to contribute to a more sustainable and inclusive future, while supporting organisations in achieving their impact goals.”

— André Hoffmann

Apply & Learn More

Apply deadline: before mid May 2026

Explore all internship offers: intent-for-change.zohorecruit.eu/jobs/Careers

More information & FAQs: www.intent-for-change.org/iip

Sister Programme — Global Internship Programme at Roche (#IP2TIS)

Also open for applications — our sister programme, the Global Internship Programme at Roche (#IP2TIS), offers 65+ fully funded positions across 20+ countries.

Programme page: go.roche.com/ip2tis_26

Live jobs: go.roche.com/ip2tis26_jobs

LinkedIn post: View on LinkedIn

The post InTent Internship Programme (IIP) 2026: Fully Sponsored Summer Internship for Students Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Huduma Fellowship 2026 for Young Leaders in Uganda’s Public Sector
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Huduma Fellowship is now open to applications, offering a transformative opportunity for early-career professionals in Uganda’s public and civic sectors. Designed to nurture ethical, effective, and forward-thinking leaders, the programme equips participants with the skills, values, and networks needed to strengthen public service and drive meaningful change. A joint initiative of the LéO Africa […]

The post Apply Now: Huduma Fellowship 2026 for Young Leaders in Uganda’s Public Sector appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Huduma Fellowship is now open to applications, offering a transformative opportunity for early-career professionals in Uganda’s public and civic sectors. Designed to nurture ethical, effective, and forward-thinking leaders, the programme equips participants with the skills, values, and networks needed to strengthen public service and drive meaningful change.

A joint initiative of the LéO Africa Institute and Konrad-Adenauer-Stiftung, the Huduma Fellowship aims to build a community of principled leaders committed to integrity, accountability, and service.

About the Huduma Fellowship

The Huduma Fellowship is a one-year leadership development programme tailored for individuals working in public service and civic spaces. Through a combination of residential seminars, mentorship, and peer learning, fellows engage deeply with the challenges and opportunities shaping governance and public leadership in Uganda.

Participants will join a network of like-minded professionals who are equally dedicated to improving systems, institutions, and communities.

Fellowship Structure

The programme is structured around four key phases, each designed to build critical leadership competencies:

1. Foundational Leadership

Fellows begin by developing self-awareness and clarifying their personal leadership values. This phase focuses on identity, purpose, and the inner work required for effective leadership.

2. Public Service Ethics and Governance

Participants explore ethical leadership within the context of public institutions, learning how to navigate complex governance environments with integrity and accountability.

3. Policy and Implementation

This phase emphasizes practical skills in translating ideas into action. Fellows learn how to design, implement, and evaluate policies and initiatives within their sectors and communities.

4. Graduation and Capstone Project

The fellowship culminates in a capstone project, where participants present solutions rooted in real-world challenges they have identified in their work or communities.

Why Apply?

The Huduma Fellowship is more than a training programme—it is a platform for shaping the future of public service in Uganda. Fellows gain the tools to lead with integrity, the confidence to address complex challenges, and the community to support their continued growth.

If you are committed to building a culture of principled public service and making a lasting impact in your sector, the Huduma Fellowship offers a unique pathway to grow as a leader and changemaker.

What to Expect

Over the course of the programme, fellows will benefit from:

  • Intensive residential seminars
  • Mentorship from experienced leaders and practitioners
  • Peer-to-peer learning and collaboration
  • Exposure to real-world governance and policy challenges
  • Opportunities to build lasting professional networks

Eligibility Criteria

The Huduma Fellowship is open to applicants who meet the following requirements:

  • Ugandan nationals or residents aged 22–35
  • Currently working in government, civil service, non-profit organizations, civil society, or other civic institutions
  • Demonstrated commitment to ethical leadership and public service

Application Process

The application process is completed in four stages:

  1. Personal Information
  2. Professional Background
  3. Application Questions
  4. Final Submission

Applicants are encouraged to provide thoughtful and authentic responses that reflect their leadership journey, values, and aspirations.

APPLY HERE

Deadline is 30th April 2026

Discover similar Fellowship Opportunities Here.

The post Apply Now: Huduma Fellowship 2026 for Young Leaders in Uganda’s Public Sector appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Cumberland Lodge Fellowship 2026–2028 for Doctoral Students and Early Career Professionals
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Cumberland Lodge Fellowship is a prestigious two-year programme designed for doctoral students and early career professionals who are passionate about tackling social division and building more inclusive communities. Each year, 10 Fellows are selected to join this transformative experience focused on leadership, dialogue, and social impact. Why Apply for the Cumberland Lodge Fellowship? This […]

The post Apply Now: Cumberland Lodge Fellowship 2026–2028 for Doctoral Students and Early Career Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Cumberland Lodge Fellowship is a prestigious two-year programme designed for doctoral students and early career professionals who are passionate about tackling social division and building more inclusive communities. Each year, 10 Fellows are selected to join this transformative experience focused on leadership, dialogue, and social impact.

Why Apply for the Cumberland Lodge Fellowship?

This flexible programme is designed to complement your academic or professional journey while equipping you with essential leadership and engagement skills. Fellows benefit from:

  • High-level networking with policymakers, academics, and industry leaders
  • Skills development in public engagement, interdisciplinary collaboration, and ethical leadership
  • Mentorship opportunities through a partnership with the Windsor Leadership Trust
  • A £300 Personal Development Grant to support projects that promote social cohesion
  • Access to a global alumni network committed to driving positive social change

Who Can Apply?

Applications are open to individuals resident in Greater London who fall into one of the following categories:

Postgraduate Fellows

  • Currently enrolled in a doctoral programme at a London university

Industry Fellows

  • Within five years of starting your career
  • Particularly encouraged: professionals in tech, AI, finance, the charity sector, and creative industries

Selection Criteria

Applicants are assessed based on:

  • Leadership potential
  • Intellectual curiosity
  • Alignment with Cumberland Lodge’s mission
  • Demonstrated commitment to social cohesion

Fellowship Structure

The programme is structured over two years, combining retreats, events, and practical contributions.

First Year

  • Attend the Annual Fellows’ Retreat (September)
  • Participate in at least one workshop or conference
  • Contribute a blog post or engage on the community platform (Circle)

Second Year

  • Attend the Annual Fellows’ Retreat (September)
  • Participate in at least one workshop or conference
  • Produce a Dialogue & Debate webinar or podcast (individually or collaboratively)

Additional Benefits

Fellows also gain access to:

  • Ongoing mentorship and facilitation training
  • Opportunities to take on leadership roles during Lodge events
  • Free entry to events such as Cumberland Conversations and the London Dialogue
  • Coverage of meals, accommodation, and London travel expenses for required activities

Ambassadorial Role

As a Fellow, you will act as an ambassador for Cumberland Lodge, promoting its mission within your networks and helping to build sustainable partnerships. Annual reunions and retreats provide continued engagement and collaboration opportunities.

How to Apply

To apply for the 2026–2028 Fellowship:

  1. Download and complete the relevant application form
  2. Submit your application along with your CV via email

Email: programmeteam@cumberlandlodge.ac.uk

Key Dates

  • Application Deadline: 9:00 AM, Monday 1 June 2026
  • Interviews (online via Microsoft Teams): 30 June or 1 July 2026

Note: Due to high application volumes, feedback is not provided to unsuccessful applicants. If you do not hear back by 15 June 2026, please assume you have not been shortlisted.

Application Documents

  • Cumberland Lodge Fellowship Application Pack 2026–28
  • Doctoral Fellowship Application Form 2026–28
  • Industry Fellowship Application Form 2026–28

The Cumberland Lodge Fellowship offers a rare opportunity to develop leadership skills, expand your professional network, and contribute meaningfully to addressing social challenges. If you are committed to fostering inclusive communities and driving social change, this programme could be a powerful next step in your journey.

Discover similar Fellowship opportunities Here. 

The post Apply Now: Cumberland Lodge Fellowship 2026–2028 for Doctoral Students and Early Career Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future 2026 in Ghana (Full & Partial Funding Available)
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Apply for the 2026 Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future in Accra, Ghana The Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future 2026 is now open for applications. This fully immersive training program will take place from 18 August to 28 August 2026 in Accra, Ghana, bringing together professionals and changemakers across Anglophone Africa to […]

The post Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future 2026 in Ghana (Full & Partial Funding Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Apply for the 2026 Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future in Accra, Ghana

The Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future 2026 is now open for applications. This fully immersive training program will take place from 18 August to 28 August 2026 in Accra, Ghana, bringing together professionals and changemakers across Anglophone Africa to strengthen governance in the extractive and energy sectors during a critical era of global energy transition.

Hosted by ACEP with support from NRGI under the Anglophone Africa Extractive Industries Knowledge Hub (AFREIKH), this one-week intensive summer school is designed to close knowledge gaps, build leadership capacity, and equip participants with practical tools for managing Africa’s natural resources more effectively.

A limited number of full and partial scholarships are available for selected applicants.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Africa’s extractive industries remain central to economic growth, public revenue generation, and foreign exchange earnings. However, many countries continue to face governance challenges such as:

  • Weak institutional systems
  • Revenue leakages
  • Limited local value addition
  • Unequal distribution of resource benefits
  • Poor community outcomes
  • Environmental and social concerns

As global markets shift toward cleaner energy systems, Africa must develop new governance models that ensure a just, inclusive, and sustainable energy transition.

This summer school provides the knowledge, networks, and policy insights needed to help shape that future.

Program Details

  • Program Name: Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future 2026
  • Dates: 18 August – 28 August 2026
  • Location: Accra, Ghana
  • Format: In-person
  • Time: Starts 9:00 AM | Ends 9:00 AM WAT
  • Funding: Full Scholarships, Partial Scholarships, Self-Funded Options Available

What Participants Will Learn

Participants will engage in high-level discussions, practical sessions, and policy analysis on the evolving dynamics of Africa’s extractive industries and energy systems.

Core Topics Include:

  • Energy transition under fiscal and governance constraints
  • Maximizing economic benefits in energy transitions
  • Environmental and Social Governance (ESG) in extractives
  • Community outcomes and benefit sharing
  • Just transition frameworks
  • Geopolitics and strategies for resource mobilization
  • Sustainable governance of natural resources

Exclusive Conference Access

Participants will also attend the Future of Energy Conference (FEC), where they will explore innovative solutions, strategic partnerships, and forward-thinking approaches for Africa’s sustainable energy future.

Benefits of the Summer School

Successful applicants will receive access to:

  • World-class training from sector experts
  • Exposure to cutting-edge policy discussions
  • Networking with professionals across Africa
  • Practical governance and leadership skills
  • Participation in the Future of Energy Conference
  • Mentorship and professional development opportunities
  • Membership in a vibrant alumni network
  • Full or partial scholarship opportunities

Eligibility Criteria

To apply, applicants must:

  • Be a citizen of an Anglophone African country
  • Hold a role in an institution working in extractives, energy, governance, media, academia, parliament, or related sectors
  • Have at least three years of relevant professional experience
  • Demonstrate strong interest in natural resource governance and energy transition issues
  • Be available to participate fully in the entire program
  • Have strong written and spoken English skills

Required Documents

Applicants must prepare:

  • Completed application form
  • Curriculum Vitae (CV)

Program Fee

The standard participation fee is USD 2,500, which covers:

  • Tuition
  • Accommodation
  • Meals
  • Local transportation
  • Program logistics

Note: International flights and visa costs are not included.

Scholarship Options

A limited number of scholarships will be awarded competitively after the review process.

Applicants can choose one of the following funding options:

  • Fully Funded (Scholarship Required)
  • Partially Funded (Covers tuition, meals, and local transportation)
  • Self-Funded / Organization-Funded

Application Deadline

Deadline: 8 May 2026

Early application is strongly recommended due to limited scholarship slots.

How to Apply

Interested candidates should complete the official application process through the organizers’ platform.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more summer school opportunities, click here. 

Final Thoughts

The Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future 2026 is an outstanding opportunity for African professionals who want to drive better governance, sustainable development, and inclusive growth in the continent’s energy and extractive sectors.

If you are passionate about shaping Africa’s future in the era of energy transition, this program is for you.

The post Summer School on Managing Africa’s Extractive Future 2026 in Ghana (Full & Partial Funding Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
